name: Child Rebel Soldier
description: In 2007, what was initially intended to be a solo Radiohead mashup mixtape by Lupe Fiasco would evolve into a collaborative record between himself, Kanye West, and Pharrell Williams following the release of their debut song as "Child Rebel Soldier", "Us Placers". By 2010, the album had been scrapped in its entirety according to Lupe, though 14 years later in 2024 Lupe would attempt to revive the group as a solo project, though again was likely cancelled this time on account of failing to gain sample clearances for the entire record.

name: G.O.O.D. Fridays
description: GOOD Fridays was a weekly free music release by Kanye West that led up to the release of My Beautiful Dark Twisted Fantasy in November 2010. The name was reused for some singles that preceded The Life of Pablo in February 2016, and once more to refer to seven-track albums that were executive produced by Ye and released weekly in summer 2018. Ye planned to bring back this concept once more in April 2022, and release 4 finished singles from Donda 2. These plans were canceled for unknown reasons, and in 2023, this information was leaked along with the finished tracks meant to be released.

tracks.name: Monster (Ft. Bon Iver, JAY-Z, Nicki Minaj & Rick Ross)
Runaway Love (Kanye West Remix) by Justin Bieber (Ft. Kanye West & Raekwon)
G.O.O.D. Friday (Ft. Big Sean, Charlie Wilson, Common, Kid Cudi & Pusha T)
Lord, Lord, Lord (Ft. Charlie Wilson, Raekwon, Swizz Beatz & Yasiin Bey)
So Appalled (Ft. CyHi, JAY-Z, Pusha T, RZA & Swizz Beatz)
Christian Dior Denim Flow (Ft. John Legend, Kid Cudi, Lloyd Banks, Pusha T & Ryan Leslie)
Don't Stop! by Child Rebel Soldier
Take One For The Team (Ft. CyHi, Keri Hilson & Pusha T)
Don't Look Down (Ft. Big Sean, Lupe Fiasco & Yasiin Bey)
The Joy by JAY-Z & Kanye West (Ft. Curtis Mayfield)
Looking for Trouble (Ft. Big Sean, CyHi, J. Cole & Pusha T)
Christmas in Harlem (GOOD Fridays Version) (Ft. Big Sean, Cam'ron, CyHi, Jim Jones, Musiq Soulchild, Pusha T, Teyana Taylor & Vado)

name: Untitled 2011 Solo Album
description: According to West himself, alongside what would have been the sessions for Watch The Throne and Cruel Summer, he had also been working on his own solo album that was set to release Summer 2011. Its first single "Theraflu" wouldn't release until over a year past its initial announcement date, though by May this album had been scrapped in favor of the album below. NOTE: The cover is for "Cold", not for the album.

tracks.name: Why Does Summer Have To End

name: Kids See Ghosts II
description: Following the release of KIDS SEE GHOSTS with Kid Cudi, Cudi would go on several interviews throughout 2018 and 2019 to confirm the two had been working together on a sequel to the record. Several songs for were recorded for the song through the Yandhi era, most notably "Thank You (Vol. 2)" which would later be handed down to Ty Dolla $ign for his album Featuring Ty Dolla $ign. It's unclear when exactly sessions for the album had been put to a halt, though the project had been indefinitely scrapped by 2022 when West had began feuding with Kid Cudi over his affiliation with Pete Davidson and Cudi responding by calling West a "dinosaur".

name: CARTI YE
description: Kanye West and Playboi Carti would meet up some time during late-2023 for the "2024" music video shoot and somewhere around this time would begin working on a collaborative album of their own amidst Carti's involvement in the VULTURES sessions. On March 11, 2025, Ye posted a picture of an album cover, which featured himself and Playboi Carti. 4 days later, Ye confirmed that the cover was for an album by Ye and Carti, titled CARTI YE, and that it will not release because Ye's features for "WE NEED ALL DA VIBES" and potentially "FOMDJ" had been removed from the final release of Carti's latest album at the time, MUSIC. The album was seemingly intended to be a playlist of previous already existing collaborations between the two made for their own respective projects.

name: World Record Holders
description: A planned compilation mixtape by The Go Getters (Kanye West, GLC, Timmy G, and Arrowstar) was privately released as a promotional CD in 1999, though never publicly released. Almost every song has been leaked or released, but the specific versions featured on the CD have not. Tracks 11–22 are said to feature commentary from Kanye. It serves as the first unreleased project related to Kanye West.

tracks.name: Nothing's Gonna Stop Me (by Go Getters)
Ghetto Senorita (by Christina) feat. Kanye West, Shaila G
All I Have (by Kanye West) feat. Cristina, Taji, Mikkey Halsted
Foolish Game (by Mikkey Halsted) feat. M. Taji
No Luv / No Love (by Go Getters)
Fight With the Best (by Kanye West) feat. Mikkey Halsted, Rhymefest
On 10 in a Benz (by Rhymefest) feat. Kanye West
Ghetto (by D-Dot) feat. Carl Thomas, Raekwon
Is It You? (Deja Vu) (Remix) (by Made Men) feat. Big Pun, Cardan, Ma$e
You Made Me (by Harlem World, Nas, Carl Thomas)
Let My N****s In (by Go Getters) [Bonus]

name: 808s & Heartbreak at the Hollywood Bowl
description: In 2015 during the sessions for Kanye West's album "SWISH", Kanye would begin work with Caroline Shaw to create an updated version of his 2008 album "808s & Heartbreak". All the songs were met with additional instrumentation done from Caroline Shaw who herself had recorded 4 features across the album. The updated versions of the songs were played at Kanye's show at the Hollywood Bowl throughout the 25th and 26th of September of that year and had been intended for release though the only song to be met with an official release from the revamped copy of the album was "Say You Will" as a SoundCloud exclusive.

tracks.name: See You in My Nightmares (feat. Lil Wayne)

name: Waves
description: "Waves" is an unreleased album by Kanye West that dates back to early-2016. These are the last sessions spanning from Yeezus II in 2014 to Waves before the album had shifted directions and been turned into The Life Of Pablo.

name: Collaboration with Mikkey Halsted
description: According to Mikkey Halsted, Kanye produced an entire album for him, which helped Mikkey join the Cash Money Records. However, the album was never released due to the label's dislike for Kanye. Some of the songs on the album include "Ghetto Life", "Superstar", "Foolish Game", and "Who Am I?".

name: Collaboration with Miss Criss
description: Ye is said to have produced an entire album for Miss Criss, Mikkey Halsted's sister, though it never released.

name: Collaborative Version of Man on the Moon: The End of Day
description: In a 2009 Rap Radar interview, Kid Cudi would confirm that Kanye was supposed to be a guardian character for Cudi during his debut studio album, Man on the Moon: The End of Day. This means that West was intended to have guest vocals on every song. It's unconfirmed and unlikely whether any of it was actually recorded or not.

name: My Beautiful Dark Twisted Fantasy (Deluxe)
description: According to HipHopDX, there was a planned deluxe for West's fifth studio album, My Beautiful Dark Twisted Fantasy. Very little is known about this deluxe and "Pure" as of now is the only known song to have been confirmed for it.

name: Collaboration with CyHi (2015)
description: In 2015, CyHi revealed that he and Kanye were planning to release a collaborative album which he compared to a "Dr. Dre and Eminem" type album. However, the album never released.

name: CombYe (with Diddy)
description: It's been stated that a collaborative EP with rapper and producer Sean Combs from 2015 had been planned and was titled "CombYe". The project was presumably intended to be a rapper/producer project given Diddy is not known to have recorded for anything around this time period though had done production for "All Day" and "Star Time".

name: Collaboration with Kendrick Lamar
description: In 2016, Ye revealed that he and Kendrick Lamar recorded 40 songs together. It is rumored that these 40 songs were all freestyles over Madlib-produced beats. The only known song to be met with an official release from these sessions is "No More Parties In LA" on The Life of Pablo.

tracks.name: No More Parties In LA (released on TLOP)

name: Collaboration with Young Thug
description: In 2016, both Ye and Young Thug had confirmed that, like with Ye and Kendrick Lamar, they had both recorded 40 songs together. The only song from these sessions to be met with an official release was "Highlights" on The Life of Pablo from the Young Thug collaborations.

name: Collaboration with Lil Uzi Vert
description: It was later confirmed that Uzi had also been working with West by insiders nearly 9 years later in 2025 and that both artists had recorded for "nearly all the same tracks", meaning that like Thug, Uzi had likely also recorded for around 40 tracks. Unlike Young Thug or Kendrick Lamar, none of their contributions made it onto The Life Of Pablo.

name: Collaborative Version of Surf
description: Chance the Rapper revealed in 2016 that West did narration for The Social Experiment's iTunes exclusive album Surf, but the group decided not to use it.

name: Cruel Autumn & Cruel Spring
description: In an interview with Complex in 2017, CyHi would go on to confirm that Cruel Winter by GOOD Music had been completed in its entirety and was just waiting on clearance from all artists involved, further going on to state there had been enough songs to also release a "Cruel Autumn" and "Cruel Spring". Whether these had actually been planned or CyHi had just said those names as a way to exaggerate the amount of songs made for the album is unclear.

name: Chiraq (2015)
description: Chiraq was developed separately for a short duration and may have been ultimately abandoned in favor of So Help Me God. There are no known recordings or sessions for this album, but a handful of cover designs and concepts for physical media have been shown, reportedly stemming from one-shot assignments given by Kanye. The project was overseen by Virgil Abloh as the creative director, with art direction contributed by Joe Perez, JJJJound, Heron Preston, and Nate Brown.

name: Collaboration with Bēkon
description: Sometime during the LOVE EVERYONE era, producer Bēkon, best known for his work on Kendrick Lamar's album DAMN., sent a ZIP file filled with several beats to Kanye West for a collaborative album. It is unlikely that Ye ever recorded on the beats. The ZIP file is currently lost.

name: Collaboration with Bump J and Sly Polaroid
description: Bump J stated that Kanye was in the process of producing a 7-track EP for him and Sly Polaroid and had been finished and intended for release as a part of the Wyoming sessions, though it was never released.

name: Collaboration with CyHi (2018)
description: In 2018, CyHi stated that he was working on a 7-track album with all tracks being produced by Ye. The only known song to be created for this album is the song "Model" which was later handed off to Ty Dolla $ign.

name: Collaboration with Talib Kweli
description: Though not explicitly confirmed to exist by either Ye or Kweli, the two had evidently also been working on a seven-track Wyoming album through leaks, Talib having recorded for several sample chops Ye had made for LOVE EVERYONE. According to notorious leaker Waterfalls, multiple beats had been sent to Talib Kweli for this record, though Kweli had only recorded for "Chop 5", "Chop 7", "Chop 8", and "Chop 11".

name: Collaboration with Valee
description: After hearing Chicago rapper Valee's now-scrapped verse on the song "I Love It", Ye expressed desire to make a 7-track project with him. The album has been confirmed to have no work done for it.

name: KIDS SEE GHOSTS (Orchestral Edition)
description: It has been confirmed through leaks that, like Late Orchestration, there had been plans to put out an orchestral remix of KIDS SEE GHOSTS with Kid Cudi and potentially ye.

name: Collaborative Version of UTOPIA
description: It was revealed in 2025 that there was a copy of UTOPIA made in 2020 that Ye had executively produced for as well as recorded for every track, including "Best Day", "Big Champ", and "Delresto".

name: Collaboration with SAINt JHN and Buju Banton
description: SAINt JHN stated in an interview that Ye wanted to executively produce an album between him, SAINt, and Buju Banton. In September and October 2020, the trio had two studio sessions together for DONDA: WITH CHILD, working on songs such as "24", "Believe What I Say", "Heaven And Earth", and "Stand United". It is likely that the idea for the now-scrapped collaborative album came from these sessions.

name: Collaboration with Future
description: According to producer 88-Keys, prior to Donda 2 Kanye and Future had been working together on a collaborative tape that would eventually turn into Future executively producing for Donda 2. Besides Keep It Burning, Maintenance, and Pablo, it is unknown what else Future had recorded for.

name: Collaborative Version of Almost Healed
description: In an interview with DJ Akademiks, Lil Durk stated that Ye "tweaked with the beats" and added "vocals behind some beats" to various songs on his album Almost Healed, but none of it was used. It is totally plausible and likely (due to Durk indirectly claiming it) that Ye worked on all of the tracks. Leaker Pop (who posted the "Pelle Coat" snippet) also said that he has all the Ye versions of the Almost Healed songs he did.

name: Collaboration with Freddie Gibbs
description: According to Freddie Gibbs in an interview with Bootleg Kev in 2026, after meeting each other through Ye's wife at the time Bianca Censori during the YEBU sessions the two had played with the idea of a collaborative album between them.

name: Collaboration with 070 Shake
description: During Ye and Ty's Haikou listening party for their album VULTURES 2, Ye would call out to 070 Shake, teasing a collaborative record between the two and telling Shake to get into contact with him so they could "finish the album". Besides "SKY CITY" and "Vagabond" which had been taken without Shake's knowledge, it's unclear what else the two had worked on in that time period.

name: DONDA 3
description: In early 2025, following Ye's infamous X (fka Twitter) rant, West would share a Tweet asking if his fans would be more interested in hearing a Donda 3 or a VULTURES 3 with Ty Dolla $ign. With the majority consensus being they would rather hear a third installment to Donda instead, West would begin teasing a third installment to the trilogy, even releasing a semi-finished DONDA 2 onto streaming platforms. When Digital Nas had been asked about the song "LET GO" that had originally been made for VULTURES 2, he stated that the song was going to be on Donda 3, Ye later quote tweeting a clip of him saying this on stream with the caption DONDA 3.

name: YAGE
description: YAGE was a rage rap centric album concept, in process during 2025. It's speculated the idea began when Ohio rapper Trippie Redd had went to Japan in March of 2025 to work with Ye. On June 11th, 2025, Digital Nas confirmed on Reddit that the album was merely an idea and never fully materialized.

name: Collaboration with NBA YoungBoy
description: According to trusted sources, the songs "ALIVE" and "MOTION" were brought back in 2026, not for YAGE or CUCK but for an unrelated collaborative album between him and YoungBoy Never Broke Again, likely consisting of YoungBoy scraps such as "WOLVES CRY" that Ye would record atop.

name: Collaboration with Young Moose
description: In the same 2025 interview Young Moose would confirm he had been signed by Ye, he would state he had been working on two albums; an album "for the streets" and an album with Ye. It's likely this is meant to be a 7-track album produced by Ye, similarly to the NEVER STOP EP by King Combs.

name: Moonwalk
description: The earliest known draft for an album by North West made in collaboration with her father Kanye West. Though entirely unheard, the album was made when North was 9 years old and was described to frequently sample vocals of North's grandmother Donda West and the track list known to exist shown twelve tracks, though more had possibly been made. The album would be scrapped in favor of her father's album Donda (2021).

name: Watch The Throne 2
description: In September 2018, Kanye randomly teased a potential sequel to his and Jay-Z's collaborative album, Watch The Throne, on Twitter. Justin Laboy also revealed that Kanye and Jay were working on new music, and Watch The Throne 2 would be releasing by the end of 2021. However, nothing ever came from this. Ye also teased the album in his infamous interview with Drink Champs in October 2022, although it is likely that he was just joking. Jay-Z hasn't commented on the album once.

name: Collaboration with Travis Scott
description: Collaboration with Travis Scott refers to a potential upcoming joint album between Ye and Travis Scott. The speculation follows a feud that unfolded during Ye's Twitter rant in early 2025, where he called out both Playboi Carti and Travis Scott. Since then, however, Carti and Scott have each given shout-outs to Ye during their live shows.

name: Collaboration with Ty Dolla Sign
description: According to Ty in an interview, long before the creation of VULTURES, Ty had been working on an album with Ye in 2018 amidst the Wyoming sessions.[24] It's very likely Ye had been executively producing a 7-track copy of Ty's album Dream House. It's unclear if the album was intended to be in collaboration with Ye similar to KIDS SEE GHOSTS or with Ye on the production similar to the other releases as while Ye does contribute vocals to every known track for the album they're all said to have been reference tracks and most of the album's production is accredited to BoogzDaBeast and not Ye.

name: FREE SEAN
description: Big Sean was seen in Uganda with West and his family in 2018. Big Sean would also confirm he had been in Uganda working with Ye on Yandhi. In Ye's Ugandan notebook posted by YesJulz it was written 'Do Sean's Album Free Sean' which was intended to be a 7-track album for the Wyoming album run, though Big Sean would confirm very little had been recorded in another interview as he had not felt inspired. The songs 'Lucky Me' and 'Hurricane' originate from these sessions.
